run_summary: Portfolio up +2.8% in unrealized PnL with three open positions. NTLA and PTCT remain intact on clinical/M&A timelines (day 24 and day 22 respectively); both healthcare names supported by sector tailwinds. AEIS re-flagged today as UNDERDONE after a -13.2% five-day drawdown followed by +5.3% bounce—thesis is consistent with the original entry but portfolio already owns it. New flags include AXSM (OVERDONE on light-volume sympathy), JBLU (RATIONAL on oil tailwind, below-average volume), VECO (OVERDONE semi-equipment divergence, -23% in 5 days with no 8-K), and a duplicate AEIS flag. Given current sector limits and position sizing, only VECO qualifies for exploratory entry.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; possible customer concentration or memory capex fear
entries.thesis: VECO down while SMH +2%, TSM +4%, AVGO +3.7% is a striking divergence. Stock is down ~23% over five sessions with no 8-K and no earnings imminent — this is either late-cycle capitulation on an already-broken chart or specific customer/OEM concern (Veeco sells to memory/advanced packaging). Against this semi tape, if there's no company-specific bad news, the disconnect creates asymmetric bounce potential. Low volume (0.62x) on today's drop suggests forced/passive selling rather than fresh negative info.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K, preannouncement, or news of a major customer (Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC) cutting orders would validate the underperformance as fundamental. Also: if ASML/AMAT commentary this week flagged advanced packaging or MOCVD weakness, VECO drop is justified.
entries.reasoning: VECO flagged OVERDONE conf 3: down -23.3% over five days, -5.34% today, while SMH +2%, TSM +4%, AVGO +3.7%. No 8-K, no earnings imminent. This is a striking chart divergence: small-cap semi-equipment name bleeding while the broad semi tape screams higher. Low volume (0.62x) on today's drop suggests forced/passive selling rather than informed repricing. Thesis is asymmetric: either (a) VECO has a specific customer/memory-capex reason to underperform, in which case an 8-K will emerge and clarify, or (b) the divergence is stretched on passive/ETF selling and overshoots on reversion. This is a textbook exploratory play—real catalyst risk (8-K, customer disclosure), well-articulated thesis, clear confirmation signals (peer divergence persists, no 8-K emerges = fade candidate; 8-K + bad news = thesis broken). My OVERDONE conf 3 hit rate is 51%, not great, but the thesis here is unusually clean and the risk/reward is asymmetric on the divergence. Size at exploratory (6% of equity = ~$60), which allows a real test without overcommitting if the 8-K kills the trade.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis relies on the absence of an 8-K as evidence of overshooting, but a -23.3% five-day decline without disclosure could equally signal that informed sellers (insiders, smart money) are already front-running a forthcoming announcement.
red_team.critique: Haiku frames low volume (0.62x) as evidence of 'forced/passive selling' rather than informed repricing, but this inference is backward: if smart money knew a customer problem was coming, they would sell through the low-volume tape to minimize slippage—exactly what we observe. The divergence with SMH, TSM, and AVGO is real, but Haiku offers no mechanism to distinguish between (a) VECO is fundamentally broken and insiders know it, and (b) VECO is oversold on technicals. The 'no 8-K yet' is offered as reassurance, but the absence of disclosure is also consistent with the bad news arriving after market close or in a pre-emptive customer announcement. Haiku's own 51% hit rate on OVERDONE trades at 3-confidence is barely above flip-a-coin, and this entry point (already down -23% in five days) may be late in the repricing cycle rather than early.

entries.name: Axsome Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with XBI selloff; no company-specific catalyst identified
entries.thesis: AXSM is down materially on only 0.67x average volume with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. A -6% print on light volume in a commercial-stage name (not a binary clinical name) inside an XBI-wide selloff typically overshoots because there are no natural buyers on a quiet-tape biotech down-day. Absent a catalyst, this is beta-driven overshoot rather than fundamental repricing.
entries.what_kills: A subsequent 8-K revealing Auvelity/Symbravo prescription weakness, label issue, or competitive setback — or if volume spikes late-day on a leaked headline.
entries.reasoning: AXSM flagged OVERDONE conf 3 on -6% move, -4.3% five-day decline into light volume (0.67x) with no 8-K. Thesis is sector-driven sympathy (XBI selloff) with no company-specific catalyst URL cited. While the logic (sub-average-volume biotech overshoots in sector selloffs) is sound in concept, the setup is pure beta reversion dependent on XBI stabilization. No named mechanism, no catalyst with a timestamp or URL, and confidence is only 3. My OVERDONE conf 3 hit rate is 51%—not strong enough to justify capital on a liquidation play with no identified catalyst. Wait for XBI to stabilize and AXSM to show actual reversal before entry.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Oil -2% on Iran de-escalation, benefits fuel-heavy carriers
entries.thesis: JBLU +6% and ALK +5.5% into oil -2% is a classic jet-fuel-cost tailwind trade. Volume actually below average (0.77x), consistent with algorithmic sector-rotation rather than a specific JBLU catalyst. Magnitude is reasonable given JBLU's high fuel-cost sensitivity and low share price gearing.
entries.what_kills: Iran headlines reverse and oil spikes back up; JBLU-specific bad news (route cuts, downgrade)
entries.reasoning: JBLU flagged RATIONAL conf 3: +6.09% on oil -2% Iran de-escalation, with ALK +5.5% correlated. Catalyst URL provided and thesis is clean (fuel-cost tailwind, algorithmic sector rotation). However, volume is below average (0.77x), suggesting this is already well-understood flows rather than a fresh structural repricing. Five-day move is unavailable, so cannot assess whether JBLU is extended into this move. Magnitude (+6%) on sub-average volume in a day-traders' sector rotation does not yet warrant committed capital. RATIONAL conf 3 thesis is well-articulated, but entry timing is not compelling. Watch for oil to hold through next trading day and JBLU to show consolidation or fresh legs higher before entering.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.
entries.thesis: AEIS -13.2% over 5 days into today's +5.3% is a partial recovery on light volume (0.69x). If the prior week's selloff was tied to a peer-driven derating without a company-specific catalyst, the bounce has room to run as SMH breaks out. The subdued volume suggests forced sellers are gone but buyers haven't fully returned.
entries.what_kills: A delayed 8-K reveals a company-specific reason for the 5-day weakness (guide cut, customer loss); SMH rolls over
entries.reasoning: AEIS re-flagged as UNDERDONE conf 3, identical setup to the position already in portfolio (entry at day 0, currently -1.77% unrealized). The new flag describes the same thesis: -13.2% five-day drawdown + +5.3% bounce on light volume into SMH strength. While the thesis logic (forced sellers clearing, bounce has room) is sound, there is NO genuinely new information since the original entry. The five-day drawdown and today's bounce were already underway; no new 8-K, no new catalyst, no validation of the original premise. Adding to AEIS would be essentially doubling down on the same bet without fresh confirmation. Current position is already exposed to this setup at 2% of equity. Skip the new flag and await thesis confirmation (multi-day continuation higher, confirmed SMH strength) before considering an add.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI selloff and gene-editing sub-sector weakness
entries.thesis: BEAM is down materially on essentially average volume (1.09x) with no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, and no company-specific news surfaced. The move is consistent with high-beta gene-editing names amplifying an XBI down-day; CRSP moving similarly reinforces this is a sub-sector unwind, not idiosyncratic. Magnitude looks proportional to XBI beta.
entries.what_kills: If a BEAM-specific pipeline setback (e.g., clinical hold, trial pause) emerges in filings later today or tomorrow — that would reclassify as UNDERDONE-down.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: Unknown — sustained underperformance vs. defense peers with no visible trigger
entries.thesis: MRCY is down materially today and down substantially over five sessions while ITA is roughly flat and XLI is green. Volume is below average (0.64x), which is unusual for a name in a fifth day of decline — typically capitulation brings volume. No 8-K, no news surfaced. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+: the isolation from sector suggests idiosyncratic bad news not yet visible (bearish), but the low volume and absence of any filing after multi-day weakness argues against a fresh catalyst (potentially exhausted).
entries.what_kills: A snap-back rally on no news would suggest the multi-day weakness was purely technical/positioning.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Profit-taking on recent run compounded by XBI selloff
entries.thesis: BBIO is down today but still up substantially over five sessions. Elevated volume (1.32x) fits a partial profit-take on a name that ran hard, amplified by XBI weakness. Magnitude looks proportional given both the sector down-day and the extended prior run.
entries.what_kills: If BBIO gives back the entire five-day gain and keeps going, an Attruby (acoramidis) commercial or competitive concern (BMY Camzyos, PFE tafamidis dynamics) may be surfacing.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.thesis: ONTO is down ~10% over 5 days heading into today; SMH +2.48% and broader semi-equipment strength (AEIS, VECO, LSCC all +5%+) explain a sympathy bounce. Volume only 1.16x average — this is a beta rebound, not a company-specific event. Magnitude tracks the sector.
entries.what_kills: A specific ONTO catalyst (contract win, guide) surfaces — would make this UNDERDONE rather than RATIONAL

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Possible Iran de-escalation / defense multiple compression, but unconfirmed
entries.thesis: AVAV is down 5.9% today and 13.9% over 5 days while ITA is flat and SPY +0.85%. Iran de-escalation headlines pressure defense demand narrative, and Anduril CEO comments about IPO 'hype cycle' may be pulling drone/autonomy multiples lower. But without a company-specific 8-K, the weight of Iran ceasefire vs. Anduril commentary vs. some unseen catalyst is not clearly one-sided enough for a directional call.
entries.what_kills: Move is purely Iran-ceasefire sympathy — mean-reverts once headlines fade; peer KTOS/RCAT show similar magnitude

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.thesis: NTLA -8.3% and CRSP -4.8% together while XBI +0.8% indicates a gene-editing subgroup catalyst — possibly a safety/data event at one of the CRISPR programs or a competitive readout. Move magnitude is consistent with a mid-severity clinical concern spread across the two most correlated names.
entries.what_kills: Isolated NTLA-specific pipeline setback — would make CRSP move sympathy-only and NTLA move justified

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide housing/rate weakness; Fed minutes showing hawkish split
entries.thesis: BLDR is down sharply over five days with no 8-K and average volume today — the move looks like continuation of a rate-driven housing derating (Fed minutes showed split on cuts, XLRE -1.65%, XLB -2.6%). The magnitude over 5 days is large but proportional to a housing demand scare; today's leg is just tape-following. Nothing here suggests mispricing either direction.
entries.what_kills: A specific negative company catalyst (guide cut, 8-K) emerging would recharacterize this as UNDERDONE bearish; conversely a rate-cut surprise would make it OVERDONE

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly sector rotation or analyst note not in feed
entries.thesis: ALGN is down sharply on a day when XLV is -1.3% and XLY is -1.78% — some sector explanation but the magnitude exceeds sector move. Volume is BELOW average, however, which argues against a discovered catalyst; more like thin-tape reaction. No 8-K. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ — bear case needs a whisper/note we don't have; bull case (fade the move) needs a reason it's overdone that isn't just 'no news.'
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a specific negative catalyst (guide-down, competitive threat news) would break the UNCLEAR framing toward RATIONAL bearish

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown; possible read-through from Trump/Walmart beef price commentary or an unreported analyst action
entries.thesis: TXRH is down while XLY is up nearly 1%, so this is name-specific not sector. No 8-K, no earnings imminent per catalyst_signals. Trump's post about Walmart cutting ground beef prices ~15% is a potential thread — beef is TXRH's core input cost, so lower beef could actually be BULLISH for margins, not bearish. Cannot construct a confident directional read from available evidence.
